violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not properly protected from potential contamination. Carrots in a pot, uncovered stored on the floor. Numerous bulk containers without lids. Uncovered food in the 3 door reach in cooler. Numerous bulk containers need to be labeled by common name on body of container.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooling of potentially hazardous food was not accomplished using proper methods. Chicken left out in the prep area in a plastic tote tempted at 82.2 degrees. Moved to walk-in freezer in metal pans 53-61 degrees 30 minutes later. Queso left to cool in plastic buckets in the walk-in cooler tempted at 97.5 degrees. Moved to metal pans and ice wands were used.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. Carboard boxes lining shelves in the walk-in cooler and in the kitchen. Cardboard can't be reused.

General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Material of food contact equipment/utensil did not meet durability characteristics or are a limited use material. Several containers using single service portion cups as scoops. Provide scoops with handles.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Food contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. The microwave above the steam table needs to be cleaned. Leak in the backside of the prep cooler on the bottom rail.--Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Cabinet underneath the coke machine in the lobby needs to be cleaned, leak from the machine lines.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Single use containers stored on the floor in the dry storage area, disposable cups stored on cardboard in the drive-thru area and the counter.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. Leaking faucet in the 3 compartment sink at the time of inspection.
Toilet facilities. — --Toilet room used by women did not contain at least one covered waste receptacle.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Exposed studs, joists or rafters were not covered with smooth, nonabsorbent and easily cleanable material. Exposed ceiling above the bag box pop area and missing ceiling tile by the drive-thru area.
Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Trim pieces above the walk-in cooler and next to the hood system were falling off.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. Spray bottles of cleaning chemicals were not labeled in the dish pit and hanging on the drainboard of the dish machine.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. Products in the prep cooler on the cooking line were not at 41 degrees or below. Temperatures ranged from 48-52 degrees. Checked 30 minutes later and the temperatures were dropping, stirred the food in the drawers, closed lids on prep cooler.
